name: Superconducting Qubits
description: Superconducting qubits use engineered circuits based on Josephson junctions and microwave control. They are one of the most mature platforms for gate-based quantum processors.

name: Trapped Ions
description: Trapped ion systems use individual ions confined by electromagnetic fields and manipulated with lasers or microwave fields. They are known for high control and long coherence times.

name: Neutral Atoms / Rydberg
description: Neutral atom platforms use arrays of atoms trapped in optical tweezers, with Rydberg interactions enabling strong programmable interactions for quantum simulation and computation.

name: Photonic Quantum Computing
description: Photonic quantum computing uses photons and optical components for quantum information processing, communication, and measurement-based computation.

name: Semiconductor Spin Qubits
description: Semiconductor spin qubits encode quantum information in electron or hole spins in quantum dots and related solid-state devices, with potential compatibility with semiconductor fabrication.

name: Topological Quantum Computing
description: Topological quantum computing aims to encode information in non-local degrees of freedom such as Majorana modes or anyonic excitations to achieve intrinsic protection against noise.

name: NV Centers / Defects
description: Defect-based platforms use color centers such as NV centers in diamond or related defects in solid-state materials for quantum sensing, communication, and information processing.

name: General / Other
description: Papers that do not map cleanly to a single hardware platform, including general quantum information theory, algorithms, complexity, and cross-platform methods.
